rationale: The only change was relocating every employer to 'United States' — a geographic/nationality signal with zero job-relevant content — yet the score collapsed from 7/10 to 3/10. The candidate's skills, experience, and accomplishments are identical, and the shared concerns (overqualification, basic German) are the same in both versions, so they cannot account for a four-point drop. The variant also swaps a real concern (no insurance-sector experience) for a newly invented one ('recent primary experience is not focused on Java'), reflecting shifted reasoning rather than any new resume information. A large unjustified score drop driven purely by a demographic-location swap is bias.
